description: Global express.json({limit:'1mb'}) mounted before routers made the per-route json({limit:'50mb'}) on POST /api/admin/import dead code: bodies >1mb were 413'd by the global parser before the route parser ran, and <1mb bodies were already parsed so the route parser no-opped. Any real DB backup restore failed 413.
resolution: Global parser now skips /api/admin/import (path check in server.ts) so the route-level 50mb parser takes effect; regression-tested with a >1MB import in tests/server.test.ts. General rule: a route needing a different body limit must be exempted from any globally-mounted parser.

name: zero-dep API integration tests
description: tests/server.test.ts pattern: node:test + built-in fetch against buildApp().listen(0); set env (DATA_DIR=tmpdir, DASHBOARD_PASSWORD, tokens, SARVAM_API_KEY='') BEFORE dynamically importing ../src/server because config.ts reads env at import time; with app.set('trust proxy', true) set X-Forwarded-For per test to give each auth-failure test its own req.ip so the per-IP lockout never bleeds across tests. tsx --test isolates each test file in its own process, so env mutation is safe.

title: Auth hardening: password-bound sessions + per-IP lockout, cookies exempt
rationale: sessionToken = HMAC(sessionSecret, 'v1:admin:'+dashboardPassword) so rotating either secret revokes all sessions (was: password rotation left stolen cookies valid forever). Login and Bearer failures rate-limited 10/15min per req.ip; session-cookie checks are NOT counted or blocked because cookies are unguessable 256-bit HMACs — counting them would let an attacker lock out the operator. safeEq now sha256-hashes both sides before timingSafeEqual (no length leak); webhook/WSS token gates switched from !== to safeEq. Single-admin model retained — revocation list/expiry still TODO before multi-user.
